title: calacinum axillare
intro: Calacinum axillare, also known as Wood's rose or wood rose, is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Calacinum axillare can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health purposes.
